paper_title: SpinyHand: Contact Load Sharing for a Human-Scale Climbing Robot
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/19_SpinyHand Contact load sharing for a human-scale climbing robot.pdf

equations_checked.notes: Both F1 and F2 numerators, the common denominator, mu signs, kp terms and x multiplier were visually checked. Retained as E1.
mu = 2(k1-k2)/(k1+k2) visually checked. Retained in E2 only under the stated two-tile force-limit matching condition.
All k1, k2 and kp factors, denominator and x2 were visually checked. Retained in E2 as a two-tile limit, not a general array formula.
Joint torque balance signs and terms J(q,c)^T fc + ft r - Kr q - pr were visually checked. Retained in E3.
Prismatic phalanx balance Px fc + fp(c,ft) - Kp d - pp was visually checked. Retained in E3.
Compact linear system A fc = b visually checked. Retained in E3 with the square/invertible condition.
Block definitions A=[J^T; Px] and b=[Kr q+pr-ft r; Kp d+pp-fp(c,ft)] visually checked. Retained in E3.
Both friction-matrix multipliers in b and M diagonal sequence 1,(1-mu),...,(1-mu)^(n-1) visually checked. Retained as E4 with the subsequent static-friction correction warning.
Finger wrench mapping wf=Wf fc visually checked. Retained in E5.
Whole-grasp wrench sum over finger wrenches visually checked. Retained in E5.

Differential grasp-wrench relation and the sums over Kfi Jfi were visually checked. Omitted as a standalone formula because the inverse-search method is summarized without reproducing intermediate stiffness algebra.
Twist transformation dx_fi=J_fi dx_g visually checked. Omitted separately; its role is preserved in the floating-wrist mechanism description.
Direction-dependent failure force fs=s(v) visually checked. Retained in E6 and paired with Appendix Eq. (A4).
Both A^-1 block vectors, the minus sign on the tendon term and decomposition fc=csys+ft cact were visually checked. Retained as E7.
Iterative fixed-wrist maximum-tendon-force expression, rotation matrix and normalized contact-force direction were visually checked. Omitted to avoid duplicating the Eq. (13)+(14) failure interface.
Fixed-wrist wrench set, tendon-force bounds, origin vector o and actuation directions vi were visually checked. Retained as E8.

Single-finger feasible wrench set Wf and fc<=fs condition were visually checked. Omitted as an independent formula; the floating-wrist feasible-set intersection and convex-hull procedure is retained in text.
Two tile force-balance equations and all signs were visually checked. Omitted as derivation support for Eq. (1).
Both pulley friction balances with (1+mu/2) and (1-mu/2) were visually checked. Omitted as derivation support for Eq. (1)-(3).
delta f_ci=(delta x_ci+delta l_ci) k_ci visually checked. Omitted as an intermediate tendon-friction increment relation; its static-friction role is preserved in E4 limitations.
Both linear force-angle fit relations, fsmax, fsmin, phi and phi_max were visually checked. Retained in E6 as an empirical tile-limit linearization.

figures_checked.reason: Robot grasp photograph is contextual and adds no equation or quantitative validation.
Grasp-mode renderings are summarized by the sloper/crimp/pinch design context.
Tile hardware layout is represented by the closing-sequence mechanism and parameter table; Fig. 5 is required for the retained load-sharing variables.
Finger hardware and tendon routing are adequately summarized in M2 and the parameter table.
Irreplaceable k1, k2, kp, local tendon tension and displacement definitions for Eq. (1)-(3); direct PDF clip visually verified.
Two-tile measured loads, five repeats, model agreement and at-least-14-percent gain are preserved textually without digitizing bars.
Four-tile friction gains are summarized; the visible seven configurations versus the text's eight is recorded as a warning.
Coordinate frames and contact locations are recoverable from E3/E5; the three retained figures cover otherwise irreducible model and validation evidence.
Floating-wrist search and convex-hull workflow is preserved as a structured mechanism; omitted to stay within the three-figure limit.
Seventy-trial tile-limit verification and linearization conclusion are preserved; no unreported numerical values were read from the plot.
Experimental apparatus details are reduced to curvature, tendon preload, sensor range/accuracy and loading mode.
Direct model-versus-measurement evidence for proximal/middle/distal shear and normal force transfer; axes, legends and caption visually verified.
Irreplaceable Fx-Fz-My wrench-space hull, mean test points and standard-deviation spheres; axes and full caption visually verified.
Failure-mode and loading-ratio trends are preserved textually; exact unreported values were not digitized.
Nonmonotonic tendon-force trend is preserved as simulation evidence, without reading new numerical optima from the curve.
Pulley-radius design trend and failure/ejection regions are summarized without extracting heat-map values.
Palm actuation and electronics packaging does not affect retained contact/load-sharing equations.
Rotary finger-base hardware detail is outside the retained mechanics.

omitted_sections: General climbing-robot history and broad multifinger grasping literature
Human rock-climbing posture background beyond the three grasp modes
Most motor, bearing, sensor-board and electronics packaging details

uncertain_items.detail: The desired mu follows from a two-tile linear-stiffness and force-limit matching argument. It is not a general optimum for a multi-tile array with unknown pulley friction directions.
The body text says all eight configurations improved by at least 24 percent, while Fig. 7 visibly presents seven labeled configurations. No missing configuration was inferred.

The text denotes failure concisely by fc>fs even though fc is a contact-force vector and fs is the direction-dependent magnitude. The evidence card treats this as magnitude along the current loading direction, not a componentwise vector inequality.
The statement that rock forces are typically two to five times greater cites reference [17] and is not a direct measurement in this paper. It is not used as a solver parameter.
The plot supports the force-angle shape and linear approximation, but the paper does not tabulate raw trial data. No precise values were digitized.
Measurements cover Fz<=0; points without uncertainty spheres are mirrored to display the full symmetric space and are not independent samples.
Tendon-force and pulley-radius trends use the model with fine-concrete limits; they are not independently validated optimum values.

known_warnings: All own experiments used coarse 36-grit sandpaper; natural rock and red-brick parameters were not measured in this paper.
The model prescribes contact locations and one resultant contact per phalanx; it does not solve surface search or individual-spine engagement.
The 6D formulation was validated only for a planar opposed two-finger Fx-Fz-My subspace, not the full four-finger hand or palm contact.
Matrix A was ill conditioned in all reported trials (cond(A)>270), so inverse contact-force estimates are sensitive to small pose errors.
Measured nonlinearity from tendon friction and backlash is not captured by the linear model; palm-base shifts reached up to 5 mm.
The first-contact-detachment failure criterion is conservative and conflicts conditionally with Section 5.7 cases where contact breakage permits continued grasping.
Two- and four-tile stiffness tests use artificial linear springs, so reported friction gains are trend evidence rather than red-brick capacity multipliers.
Poppler reported missing display fonts for Symbol and ArialUnicode, but the 13 rendered pages, all retained equations, labels and three direct PDF clips were visually legible and checked.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains tile, phalanx and whole-hand wrench-space layers.
